subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear narrative structure with logical progression through introduction, modelling, results, and conclusion
Use of headings, sub-headings, numbered equations and labelled figures providing an evident organizational framework
weaknesses: Extended Python code excerpts and digressions interrupt the flow and reduce overall conciseness
Some tables and images are awkwardly placed or lack clear labels and consistent formatting
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Coherence and organization demonstrate good logical development (band 3), but conciseness suffers from irrelevant material and repetition (band 2). Overall, the significant issues in conciseness justify a criterion score of 2.

strengths: Consistent definition of variables, clear integral notation, and correct attachment of units
Effective use of multiple representations (GeoGebra overlays, scatter plots, comparative model figures) to support understanding
Good logical structure in explanations, linking calculations directly to conclusions
weaknesses: Minor inconsistencies in terminology and units (e.g. mislabelled cross-sectional area)
Occasional missing axis labels and slight variations in notation between text and equations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All strands fall solidly in band 3: mostly consistent use of mathematical language and notation, effective representations, and good clarity. These justify a criterion score of 3.

strengths: Personal context (dog treats, own fanny-pack scenario) shows clear independent thinking
Incorporating an AI-generated trigonometric model displays creativity and initiative
weaknesses: Mathematical methods follow standard procedures, limiting outstanding innovation
Approach, while personal, does not introduce highly novel methodologies
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evidence of independent thinking, personal approach, and creativity are significant but not exceptional (band 2 across strands), supporting a criterion score of 2.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on modelling choices and limitations, with thoughtful commentary on RÂ² results
Analysis links outcomes to practical implications and acknowledges constraints
weaknesses: Reflection lacks depth in error analysis and consideration of non-functional shape modelling
Limited discussion of future improvements and comprehensive evaluation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection demonstrates thoughtful analysis and clear evaluation but does not reach the depth or critical insight of band 3; a criterion score of 2 is appropriate.

strengths: Relevant calculus, regression, and optimisation techniques applied systematically to the real-world problem
Mathematics fully commensurate with SL course level and generally accurate
weaknesses: Minor conceptual slips (e.g. misnaming areas) and reliance on software without discussing potential inaccuracies
Lack of deeper quantitative error analysis or more advanced modelling methods
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands at band 4 (relevance and level), one at band 3 (understanding and accuracy) yield an overall band 3 performance: competent and correct mathematics with minor errors, meriting a criterion score of 3.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations accompany every calculation, and conclusions are linked back to previous results, giving good clarity.  Occasional wording ambiguities and an over-long code excerpt weaken communication slightly, so the work sits comfortably in the âgood logical structure and clear communicationâ level.

Integration and algebraic manipulation are executed correctly, and the student recognises potential over-estimation and density issues, indicating good conceptual grasp.  Minor conceptual slips (misnaming cross-sectional area as surface area, relying on calculator integrals without error bounds) show that understanding, while good, is not yet âthorough and completely correctâ.

The student employs photographs, scatter plots with best-fit curves, GeoGebra overlays, tables of parameters, and schematic Canva diagrams.  These varied representations genuinely aid understanding, but axes are sometimes unlabeled and scale choices are not always explained; hence the use is effective rather than âhighly effectiveâ.

The exploration follows a clear narrative: introduction of the problem, modelling of the snack outline, volume calculation, packingâdensity experiment, and optimisation of the cylinder.  Transitions between these stages are logical and the reader can follow the argument without major effort.  Nevertheless, occasional digressions (e.g. extended discussion of software limitations or detailed quotation of Python code) interrupt the flow, so the coherence does not reach the âexcellentâ descriptor.

Using AI to generate trigonometric fits, comparing them with polynomial models, and designing a pragmatic density experiment reveal creativity and initiative.  Still, these ideas remain within expectations for a solid IA and do not attain the âexceptionalâ descriptor.

The student analyses goodness-of-fit via RÂ², compares two modelling strategies, and evaluates empirical packing evidence.  This demonstrates thoughtful analysis, but the critique is not highly probing (e.g., no discussion of numerical integration error), placing it in the âthoughtfulâ rather than âinsightfulâ level.

Outcomes are linked to the practical goal and limitations are acknowledged (over-estimation of area, software precision).  Implications for future improvement are mentioned only briefly, so the evaluation is clear but not comprehensive.

Reflection appears at decision points (choice of model, packing-density test) and a final section discussing limitations.  The commentary moves beyond description to consider why choices were made, thus providing meaningful reflection, though not deep or sustained enough for the top band.

Variables are defined, functions are written with correct powers, integrals are displayed with limits, and units are generally attached.  Minor slips (mis-labelled âsurface areaâ for cross-sectional area, inconsistent use of cmÂ²/ cm3) prevent full consistency, placing the work in the âmostly accurateâ band.

The investigation is rooted in the studentâs personal context (dog treats, own fanny-pack), and decisions such as the two storage orientations and height choice show a clear personal stamp.  The approach is clearly theirs, yet it is not especially innovative or unusual, matching the middle band.

Most material is pertinent to answering the research question, yet several passages (e.g. lengthy reflection on discovering the RÂ² statistic, full AI code dump, and two pages of Canva mock-ups) do not advance the mathematics and could be trimmed.  Repetition of background explanation also inflates the length to 19 pages, so conciseness is only âmostlyâ achieved.

Polynomial and trigonometric curve fitting, definite integrals, volume relations and optimisation are directly relevant to solving the stated problem and are applied in a systematic way.  The mathematics is used effectively, though not with the breadth or sophistication expected for the very top bands.

Selecting an everyday problem, devising personal measurements, and deciding to test packing density empirically demonstrate noticeable independent thought beyond textbook examples.  However, much of the mathematical technique (polynomial fitting, basic integration) follows standard procedures, so the evidence is significant rather than outstanding.

Headings, sub-headings, numbered equations and labelled figures give the work an unmistakable structure (introduction â methodology â results â conclusion).  Each major section is easy to locate and the logical hierarchy is evident.  However, the report occasionally merges distinct ideas into one long paragraph and some images are awkwardly placed, so the organisation is good rather than exceptional.

The calculus, regression techniques and optimisation are solidly within HL course level and are applied to a real context.  While appropriate and mostly correct, the work does not push into more advanced HL territory (e.g. multi-variable optimisation, error analysis), so âfully commensurateâ is appropriate.

annotations.comment: Consider justifying the choice of the most regular treat by quantifying variability across multiple samples to strengthen the modelâs representativeness.
Reflect more deeply on the limitations introduced by modeling a non-function shape as a single function and how this may affect volume accuracy.
Embedding the treatâs image into GeoGebra with a 1 cm:1 unit scale demonstrates effective use of multiple representations for spatial accuracy.
The title is concise and descriptive, immediately conveying the investigationâs focus and providing strong context for the reader.
The studentâs personal narrative in the introduction effectively engages the reader and demonstrates genuine personal engagement with the problem.
Terminology is inconsistent: referring to the snackâs cross-sectional area as âsurface areaâ may confuse the reader; clarify and consistently use precise terms.
Real-life measurements are used to maintain practical dimensions, showing strong relevance and application of mathematics to the context.
Explaining the use of unrounded values in GeoGebra while displaying rounded figures enhances transparency in mathematical communication.
When selecting a 4th-degree polynomial, consider including a brief derivative sign analysis to justify the choice based on stationary point requirements.
Relying on GeoGebraâs point-identification tool without discussing potential positional errors may introduce unseen inaccuracies in the fit.
Presenting the polynomial equation clearly with numbering and notation supports mathematical communication and reference.
In addition to qualitative fit remarks, include residual plots or error metrics to quantitatively assess the polynomialâs performance.
Notation for exponents and polynomial terms varies between equation presentation and superscript text; ensure consistent mathematical notation throughout.
Introduce goodness-of-fit metrics (e.g., RÂ²) earlier when fitting the upper curve to systematically compare both polynomial models.
Including an investigation of the RÂ² statistic shows thoughtful extension into statistical interpretation of curve fitting.
Systematically increasing polynomial degree and observing RÂ² progression demonstrates methodical use of mathematics to improve the model.
Relying solely on RÂ² without addressing potential overfitting or overestimated curvature may lead to inflated confidence in the model.
The 6th-degree polynomial equation is clearly stated and properly numbered, facilitating follow-through of subsequent calculations.
The visual comparison between polynomial fits (Figure 6) effectively conveys improvements in the bottom right region.
Consider using a piecewise or spline model rather than a single polynomial to reduce overestimation of the area near the ends.
The integral notation has a syntactic issue: the integrand is written as [u(x) dx â l(x)] dx instead of â«(u(x)âl(x)) dx; correct for clarity.
Defining u(x) and l(x) clearly before integration shows strong mathematical communication and prepares for precise calculation.
Introducing a trigonometric approach exhibits creativity and independent thinking in exploring alternative models.
The tableâs columns are unlabeled in the layout, and font sizes vary; ensure subtitles and units are clear for each model segment.
Lengthy Python code excerpts can disrupt flow; consider summarizing key steps in the main text and moving full code to an appendix.
Critically evaluating the bottom-left regionâs lower RÂ² demonstrates thoughtful analysis and willingness to interrogate results.
Code table formatting is overcrowded and hard to follow; simplify layout and highlight crucial algorithmic steps for reader clarity.
Enhance figures by adding axis labels and units to provide context for scale and ensure clear interpretation across representations.
Comparing polynomial and trigonometric fits side-by-side is an effective use of multiple representations to illustrate model differences.
